title: Hostages

jokes.text: I'd be terrible in a hostage situation, even as a hostage taker.
jokes.setup: Selina expressing concern about the hostage crisis
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission she'd fail at both sides of hostage-taking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's self-awareness of her incompetence, economically delivered

jokes.text: he knows I've got a bigger role in the White House now, which means I've got a bigger dick, which means he can suck it.
jokes.setup: Discussing her increased White House role
jokes.punchline: Escalating from professional power to crude sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep profanity escalation, well-integrated with Selina's character but predictable

jokes.text: That's gonna electrify DC. - Oh, it's gonna AC DC.
jokes.setup: Amy saying 'robust' will electrify DC
jokes.punchline: Dan escalating with AC/DC reference
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced and doesn't land with much impact

jokes.text: Robust is a good word. And you don't hear it often, like bumptious.
jokes.setup: Praising 'robust' as a word choice
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the obscure word 'bumptious'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - his tendency to make oddly specific but meaningless observations

jokes.text: Robust makes me think of wine. It's actually how I like my reds.
jokes.setup: Asked about the word 'robust' in crisis context
jokes.punchline: Mike immediately thinks of wine instead of military response
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mike's complete disconnect from serious political matters

jokes.text: You like your reds through a bendy straw, too, right, Mike?
jokes.setup: Mike talking about liking robust red wines
jokes.punchline: Dan mocking Mike's sophisticated wine talk with childish drinking method
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that perfectly deflates Mike's pretension with vivid, specific imagery

jokes.text: You got donut on you, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to appear sophisticated about wine
jokes.punchline: Dan pointing out Mike has donut crumbs on him
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that perfectly undercuts Mike's pretensions with physical reality

jokes.text: VP stands for 'very precious.'
jokes.setup: Gary giving advice to Cliff about taking care of Selina
jokes.punchline: Redefining VP acronym in overly precious way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment showing his devotional, slightly creepy relationship with Selina

jokes.text: Wow. Hope it comes with CliffsNotes.
jokes.setup: Gary showing Cliff the massive Veeplopedia binder
jokes.punchline: Cliff making pun on his own name
jokes.explanation: Predictable, low-hanging pun that feels forced rather than clever

jokes.text: That was a play on my name. My name is Cliff.
jokes.setup: Cliff making the CliffsNotes pun
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explaining his own joke
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - explaining your own weak joke makes it funnier through awkwardness

jokes.text: Don't do jokes, Cliff. I don't have a sense of humor and neither should you.
jokes.setup: Cliff attempting humor
jokes.punchline: Gary's deadpan admission of humorlessness while being funny
jokes.explanation: Excellent paradoxical line - Gary being funny while claiming to have no humor, perfectly in character

jokes.text: How do you make two perfectly pleasant words so irritating?
jokes.setup: Someone saying 'Good morning'
jokes.punchline: Ben's cynical observation about making pleasantries annoying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ben's world-weary cynicism, economically expressed

jokes.text: the man who can't take a leak without polling his balls.
jokes.setup: Describing someone who over-relies on polling
jokes.punchline: Escalating to crude metaphor about polling even bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Vivid, original metaphor that perfectly captures over-dependence on polling data

jokes.text: Secretary of Defense Maddox is living up to his name. He's mad as an ox.
jokes.setup: Discussing Secretary Maddox being upset
jokes.punchline: Playing on his name with 'mad as an ox'
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun but feels predictable and low-effort given the setup

jokes.text: He had a pro-strike hawk boner the size of Jonah.
jokes.setup: Describing the President's enthusiasm for military action
jokes.punchline: Comparing size of enthusiasm to Jonah (who's very tall)
jokes.explanation: Creative visual metaphor that works both as crude humor and character reference

jokes.text: There's cultures that don't even have any numbers and they do just fine.
jokes.setup: Ben expressing hatred for numbers in his job
jokes.punchline: Absurd justification using imaginary numberless cultures
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic that fits Ben's curmudgeonly character perfectly

jokes.text: Ben, it seems like you're ill-suited for your job and you should resign.
jokes.setup: Ben complaining about numbers being part of his job
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact suggestion that Ben resign
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - logical, blunt, and devastating in its simplicity

jokes.text: Yeah, precise and surgical, like your lobotomy, Kent.
jokes.setup: Kent describing military precision
jokes.punchline: Ben turning the precision description into an insult about Kent's personality
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that cleverly repurposes Kent's own words against him

jokes.text: No, that's cherry red. - Cerise. - I'm sorry, sir. I must have a very specific form of color blindness.
jokes.setup: Ben correcting Jonah about color of binder
jokes.punchline: Jonah's overly elaborate excuse for getting it wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah moment showing his tendency to over-explain simple mistakes

jokes.text: Oh, the noodle analogy. You guys are gonna love this.
jokes.setup: Kent about to launch into an elaborate noodle analogy
jokes.punchline: Ben's weary recognition that Kent is about to be insufferable
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the coming cringe, Ben's resigned tone sells it

jokes.text: I will leave.
jokes.setup: Kent beginning his elaborate noodle analogy
jokes.punchline: Ben's immediate threat to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that perfectly capture Ben's exasperation

jokes.text: Any less and they will be flaccid and damp like a lady's hair in the rain.
jokes.setup: Kent describing proper noodle preparation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, inappropriately specific comparison to women's hair
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Kent-like bizarre specificity that's both absurd and oddly vivid

jokes.text: Why are we talking about noodles? - No, let me rephrase that. Why the fuck are we talking about noodles?
jokes.setup: Ben's initial confused question about the noodle talk
jokes.punchline: Escalating to profane version of the same question
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure showing Ben's mounting frustration perfectly

jokes.text: even though my salivary glands are crying out to me like newly hatched birds.
jokes.setup: Kent explaining his patience with noodle preparation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor comparing hunger to baby birds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly bizarre Kent metaphor that's both specific and completely absurd

jokes.text: You just timed out that whole shit analogy for your little ding?
jokes.setup: Kent's elaborate noodle analogy perfectly timed to a bell
jokes.punchline: Ben's incredulous realization that Kent staged the whole thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Kent's pretentious setup, Ben's outrage is perfectly justified

jokes.text: That is fucking pathetic.
jokes.setup: Kent's elaborate staged noodle demonstration
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective capper to the sequence, Ben's disgust is palpable

jokes.text: Probably got his head stuck in her ass.
jokes.setup: Discussing Neil's absence
jokes.punchline: Gary's crude assessment of Neil's sycophantic behavior
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective, though somewhat predictable insult from Gary

jokes.text: They don't salute indoors, Gary. - They don't salute indoors, Gary.
jokes.setup: Gary commenting that Marines aren't saluting
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately repeating the correction to sound knowledgeable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - immediately parroting information to seem informed

jokes.text: And did you know in the CD player I've got Ride of the Valkyries? (CHUCKLES) Apocalypse Now.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to connect with Marines about helicopters
jokes.punchline: Awkward reference to Wagner and Vietnam War movie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Selina's awkward attempts at military banter

jokes.text: I was married to a Devil Dog, but he wasn't a Marine. He was more of a devil.
jokes.setup: Learning Marines are called Devil Dogs
jokes.punchline: Awkward overshare about her ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Good awkward oversharing moment that's very Selina - inappropriate personal details

jokes.text: Small arms means guns, ma'am. - Oh, really, Gary? Not T-rex hands?
jokes.setup: Gary condescendingly explaining military terminology
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic response about T-rex arms
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that perfectly deflates Gary's condescension with absurd comparison

jokes.text: In anger, too, I might add. Ooh, uh-oh!
jokes.setup: Maddox saying he's fired a gun before
jokes.punchline: Adding he's done it 'in anger' and Selina's nervous reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and reaction beat showing tension between characters

jokes.text: The gun could literally backfire. That's where the phrase comes from.
jokes.setup: Discussion of plan potentially backfiring
jokes.punchline: Gary's literal interpretation and etymology lesson
jokes.explanation: Good Gary moment - his tendency to be overly literal and educational

jokes.text: You could chip a veneer and I don't really want that to happen.
jokes.setup: Selina about to fire a gun
jokes.punchline: Gary's concern about cosmetic dental damage over safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary priorities - worried about Selina's appearance over actual danger

jokes.text: Here he is, Kanye West Wing.
jokes.setup: Someone entering dramatically
jokes.punchline: Combining Kanye West with West Wing reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but feels somewhat forced, though economically delivered

jokes.text: I'm the boy named Sue.
jokes.setup: Cliff explaining his role as Sue's replacement
jokes.punchline: Reference to Johnny Cash song about boy named Sue
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference that works but doesn't land with much impact

jokes.text: Cliff, when I said no jokes, that wasn't a joke.
jokes.setup: Cliff making another reference/joke
jokes.punchline: Gary's deadpan clarification about his no-jokes rule
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Gary's character while being funny itself

jokes.text: some guy in the hospital with locked-in syndrome.
jokes.setup: Discussing who's watching Sue's boring hearing
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to someone who literally can't change the channel
jokes.explanation: Darkly specific and clever - perfect Dan-style inappropriate reference

jokes.text: All right. There speaketh a pricketh.
jokes.setup: Marine's polite correction about civilian attitudes
jokes.punchline: Selina's immediate crude comeback using same speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - mirrors the setup perfectly while escalating to crude insult

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus fucking Christ!
jokes.setup: Selina firing the gun
jokes.punchline: Her shocked profane reaction to the recoil/noise
jokes.explanation: Predictable reaction but works for the character and situation

jokes.text: Fastest gun in the West Wing.
jokes.setup: Selina successfully firing the gun
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'fastest gun in the west' with West Wing reference
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun that works in context but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: With this hostage situation, I don't think you have the intelligence. As in intel, of course, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Maddox seemingly insulting Selina's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he meant intelligence as in military intel
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure that creates tension then releases it with clarification

jokes.text: Is it four or is it five? Or is it six? Or is it seven? You don't know, do you?
jokes.setup: Maddox asking about countries neighboring Uzbekistan
jokes.punchline: Selina escalating the numbers while clearly not knowing either
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's bluffing tactics, well-paced building

jokes.text: I pulled that straight out of my ass.
jokes.setup: Selina successfully answering it's five countries
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting she completely guessed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - bluffing successfully then immediately undermining herself

jokes.text: He's Iwo Jima. I'm planting a flag right on his head.
jokes.setup: Selina celebrating her victory over Maddox
jokes.punchline: Military metaphor about claiming victory on his head
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor that fits the military setting and Selina's triumphant mood

jokes.text: Oh, shit a shark.
jokes.setup: Sue being asked about the wasteful motorcade situation
jokes.punchline: Sue's panicked, nonsensical exclamation
jokes.explanation: Great panicked reaction from usually composed Sue, economically delivered

jokes.text: Robust? - Robust.
jokes.setup: Sue using 'robust' in her testimony
jokes.punchline: The word being repeated with growing concern
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the word becoming problematic, building tension

jokes.text: Okay, now she just sounds mentally ill.
jokes.setup: Sue repeatedly using 'robust' in testimony
jokes.punchline: Dan's harsh assessment of how Sue sounds
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cruel observation that's very Dan-like in its bluntness

jokes.text: I'm guessing you have one of those word-a-day calendars. And I know what the word for today is. It's robust.
jokes.setup: Sue repeatedly using 'robust'
jokes.punchline: Hessler's sarcastic explanation for the repetition
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation that calls out the obvious word repetition

jokes.text: Well, I'm guessing that you think you have a sense of humor, Mr. Hessler. Which you are wrong about, as you are about the calendar. That is my definition of robust.
jokes.setup: Hessler making sarcastic comment about word-a-day calendar
jokes.punchline: Sue's cutting comeback about his lack of humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that shows Sue's steel under pressure, well-structured burn

jokes.text: It's time to robust a nut all over this place.
jokes.setup: Mike getting excited about using 'robust' in the speech
jokes.punchline: Turning 'robust' into crude sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Crude and somewhat forced, but fits Mike's inappropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: Get your own ear, van Gogh.
jokes.setup: Dan asking Gary to get the VP's ear
jokes.punchline: Gary's artistic reference about ears
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that works well as a quick comeback

jokes.text: There goes the R-bomb.
jokes.setup: Selina using 'robust' in her speech despite the plan change
jokes.punchline: Amy treating the word like explosive ordinance
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor treating the word choice like a political bomb

jokes.text: Stick a thermometer up Washington's ass. See how much heat I just created. - Parting the cheeks.
jokes.setup: Selina asking Mike to gauge political reaction
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor and Mike's enthusiastic follow-up
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective metaphor, good character interaction

jokes.text: What's the opposite of constipated, Mike? - Relieved. - No. Relieved is not the opposite of constipated, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to comfort Selina about looking constipated in photo
jokes.punchline: Mike's logical but wrong answer about relief
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Mike's literal thinking vs actual meaning

jokes.text: VP sister act robusting the moves
jokes.setup: Newspaper coverage of Sue and Selina both using 'robust'
jokes.punchline: Headline treating them like a comedy duo
jokes.explanation: Decent media satire but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: Sue made it. - Sure. It's there, too.
jokes.setup: Looking at word cloud and seeing 'Sue'
jokes.punchline: Selina mishearing/misunderstanding as 'sure'
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay moment that doesn't land with much impact

jokes.text: Sue only has one name like Pocahontas or Hitler.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Sue's name appears bigger in word cloud
jokes.punchline: Comparing Sue to historical figures, ending with Hitler
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that takes an innocent observation to dark territory

jokes.text: Or Moby or Bigfoot.
jokes.setup: Amy's list of single-name figures
jokes.punchline: Mike adding fictional/mythical creatures
jokes.explanation: Good Mike addition that escalates to absurdist territory

jokes.text: What? You're not fucking Sue.
jokes.setup: Selina calling for Sue and getting Cliff instead
jokes.punchline: Selina's frustrated profane observation
jokes.explanation: Simple frustrated reaction that works for Selina's character

jokes.text: Start popping your popcorn. - Must Sue TV.
jokes.setup: Cliff being enthusiastic about Sue's return appearance
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic 'Must See TV' pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: It's Gary-oke time. - Gary-oke is done, my friend. It's time for Danny-oke now.
jokes.setup: Selina referring to Gary's senator prep as 'Gary-oke'
jokes.punchline: Dan claiming it's now 'Danny-oke' time
jokes.explanation: Good power struggle moment using the karaoke pun structure

jokes.text: Don't be ridiculous. I'm not famous. Well, maybe only in DC. Oh, yeah, me and Kim Kardashian. Ha ha.
jokes.setup: Sue on phone presumably being interviewed
jokes.punchline: Sue's false modesty followed by Kardashian comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sue enjoying her brief fame

jokes.text: Does aggressive trump robust? - Well, it's robustier.
jokes.setup: Comparing word strength of 'aggressive' vs 'robust'
jokes.punchline: Dan making up 'robustier' as comparative form
jokes.explanation: Good made-up word that fits the absurd word comparison discussion

jokes.text: robust is like a rock and aggressive is like paper. Oh, shoot, paper covers rock.
jokes.setup: Trying to compare word strength
jokes.punchline: Mike applying rock-paper-scissors logic and realizing his mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Mike logic that perfectly captures his confused thinking

jokes.text: His word is 'get-the-vice-president- over-here-right-now-we're-having-a-fucking-meeting.' It's all one word hyphenated.
jokes.setup: Ben sending a message through text
jokes.punchline: Amy treating the entire angry message as one hyphenated word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that treats Ben's anger as part of the word game

jokes.text: Oh, I thought you were gonna say diet, Ben.
jokes.setup: Ben saying they need to be on the same 'page'
jokes.punchline: Selina mishearing/misunderstanding as 'diet'
jokes.explanation: Minor misdirection that works for character but isn't particularly strong

jokes.text: And I don't have to answer to you, Ben. Or to Lara Croft here for that matter.
jokes.setup: Selina pushing back against Ben and Kent
jokes.punchline: Comparing Kent to video game character Lara Croft
jokes.explanation: Good insult that plays on Kent's calculated, video game-like approach

jokes.text: Listen, we all know the White House would work so much better if there wasn't a President, but there is.
jokes.setup: Discussion about working for the President
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact assessment that the President is the problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that perfectly captures the show's cynical view of politics

jokes.text: Calculated? That sounds so... - Calculated? - Pussy-ass, that's what I was gonna say.
jokes.setup: Selina objecting to 'calculated' as their word
jokes.punchline: Ben finishing her thought with crude assessment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Ben completing Selina's thought crudely

jokes.text: She fires a gun one time and she thinks she's Rommel in the desert.
jokes.setup: Selina being aggressive about military action
jokes.punchline: Ben comparing her to famous German general
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation with good historical reference, well-crafted

jokes.text: When you do that, I'm gonna start supporting gun control.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she might start 'packing heat'
jokes.punchline: Ben's threat to switch political positions if she gets a gun
jokes.explanation: Good political humor that plays on policy positions and personal fear

jokes.text: Cock-a-noodle-doo.
jokes.setup: Kent announcing they can move on the hostages
jokes.punchline: Ben's rooster call combining with earlier noodle analogy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that combines celebration with earlier absurd analogy

jokes.text: He's gonna procrasterbate forever.
jokes.setup: Discussing the President's tendency to delay decisions
jokes.punchline: Combining 'procrastinate' and 'masturbate'
jokes.explanation: Excellent portmanteau that perfectly captures the President's indecisive nature

jokes.text: This is the Vice President. - Yes, it is. - No, no, this is the Vice President.
jokes.setup: Selina calling the office and identifying herself
jokes.punchline: Cliff agreeing rather than understanding she wants to speak
jokes.explanation: Good confusion comedy showing Cliff's literal interpretation

jokes.text: Set your DVR. I'm thinking of... Okay, you hung up.
jokes.setup: Cliff getting excited about Sue's hearing return
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's talking to no one
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing Cliff's obliviousness and enthusiasm

jokes.text: Amy, this is like explaining gravity to a chicken.
jokes.setup: Cliff not understanding the calendar situation
jokes.punchline: Selina's absurd comparison about explaining concepts to animals
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd metaphor that perfectly captures frustration with stupidity

jokes.text: You have made a fuck-up the size of France and you are fucking grinning about it.
jokes.setup: Cliff grinning despite his major mistake
jokes.punchline: Selina's geographic comparison for the scale of the error
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with vivid geographic metaphor for mistake magnitude

jokes.text: I'm trying not to. I'm not sure why my face is doing this.
jokes.setup: Selina pointing out Cliff is grinning at his mistake
jokes.punchline: Cliff's helpless explanation of his involuntary facial expression
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Cliff's nervous inappropriate reaction

jokes.text: This is all Cliff's fault, that grinning fucking Ewok.
jokes.setup: Selina blaming Cliff for the scheduling conflict
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to Star Wars character
jokes.explanation: Good insult that combines callback with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Well, I feel like that porn star who had to do 200 men in one day. At least she got to lie down, right?
jokes.setup: Selina facing 33 senators to swear in
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comparison to pornographic endurance record
jokes.explanation: Shocking inappropriate comparison that escalates to even worse detail

jokes.text: I'm imagining you in a bathtub full of rose petals.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking to be thought of as cheerleader
jokes.punchline: Mike's completely unrelated romantic/sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike non-sequitur. Completely inappropriate and random imagery.

jokes.text: Think of me as a cheerleader, Mike. - Oh, God. I'm imagining you in a bathtub full of rose petals.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing his supportive role
jokes.punchline: Mike's disturbing romantic visualization
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Mike's inappropriate mental imagery

jokes.text: Did she just make an innuendo? - Yep. Yep.
jokes.setup: Selina's comment about 'wanting the big one'
jokes.punchline: Staff confirming the accidental sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that acknowledges the inappropriate subtext

jokes.text: so help me Brian. - Yes. - I am Brian. - I think you mean, 'So help you God.'
jokes.setup: Selina administering oath to Senator Brian
jokes.punchline: Getting confused and saying his name instead of God
jokes.explanation: Great confusion comedy showing Selina's frazzled mental state

jokes.text: Oh, I wish that was my cousin's name.
jokes.setup: Meeting someone named Ruby
jokes.punchline: Selina's random, nonsensical personal response
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Selina small talk showing her complete lack of social skills

jokes.text: This is the worst small talk I've ever heard. And I'm including mine in that and mine is horrible.
jokes.setup: Watching Selina's terrible small talk
jokes.punchline: Amy admitting her own small talk is bad but Selina's is worse
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that establishes the scale of Selina's awfulness

jokes.text: What in the living Lord of Christ are you doing here?
jokes.setup: Selina surprised to see someone
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good character exclamation but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: Well, that's how the cookie crumbles.
jokes.setup: Senator mentioning skiing in Vermont
jokes.punchline: Selina's completely inappropriate cliché response
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Selina's terrible small talk - inappropriate cliché

jokes.text: That's great. For us, I mean. 'Cause we're done.
jokes.setup: Learning the last senator has no family left
jokes.punchline: Amy's callous relief that means no photo ops
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Amy's political calculation over human sympathy

jokes.text: What's time o'clock? - Time o'clock.
jokes.setup: Selina saying it's 'time o'clock'
jokes.punchline: Amy's confused repetition of the nonsense phrase
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay moment that doesn't land with much impact

jokes.text: Maybe she's pregnant again. - Oh, God. Or she's menopausal. Oh, God, she's gonna want that testosterone gel. I'm out of here.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain Selina's erratic behavior
jokes.punchline: Gary's escalating horror at potential causes and treatments
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gary's intimate but horrified knowledge of Selina

jokes.text: They all leave me eventually.
jokes.setup: Senator Philip finishing his oath
jokes.punchline: His depressing observation about people leaving him
jokes.explanation: Good dark moment that adds pathos while being funny in context

jokes.text: The men get the situation room, I get the shituation room?
jokes.setup: Selina being relegated to lesser room while men go to situation room
jokes.punchline: Her crude pun on 'situation'
jokes.explanation: Good pun that captures Selina's frustration with being sidelined

jokes.text: This is so awesome. I'll bet you camera three gets a kill.
jokes.setup: Watching live military operation
jokes.punchline: Jonah treating life-or-death situation like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment showing his inappropriate gaming mindset

jokes.text: George, comments like that are incredibly inappropriate when people's lives are at stake.
jokes.setup: Maddox warning Selina not to swear during the operation
jokes.punchline: Selina lecturing about appropriateness while being inappropriate herself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Selina's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Yeah! Eggs-shak-a-laka! In your fucking face, eggs.
jokes.setup: Hearing the hostages (eggs) have been rescued
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude celebration directed at metaphorical eggs
jokes.explanation: Good absurd celebration that shows Selina's inappropriate reaction to success

jokes.text: Ashes to ashes, robust to dust.
jokes.setup: Sue's moment in spotlight being over
jokes.punchline: Playing on funeral prayer while referencing the 'robust' controversy
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that gives poetic closure to the word controversy

jokes.text: Yeah! Semper fucking fee. - Fi. - Fi. Fi, right.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to use Marine motto
jokes.punchline: Getting it wrong and being corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's fake military enthusiasm

jokes.text: Oh, so they were able to just salvage his foot?
jokes.setup: Learning Marine lost leg above the knee
jokes.punchline: Selina's confused attempt to find silver lining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment showing her inability to process bad news properly

jokes.text: I call that overshadowboxing.
jokes.setup: Dan praising Selina for overshadowing Sue's hearing
jokes.punchline: Combining 'overshadowing' with 'shadowboxing'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that captures Dan's political calculation mindset

jokes.text: The leg is just the price of admission to the place you wanted to be.
jokes.setup: Selina feeling guilty about the Marine's injury
jokes.punchline: Amy's cold political calculation about acceptable casualties
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark line that captures the show's cynical view of political ambition

jokes.text: Sure, we'll roll out the ramps.
jokes.setup: Selina suggesting they invite the injured Marine to White House
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact reference to wheelchair accessibility
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor with perfect economy, typical Amy ruthlessness

jokes.text: I guarantee you they're better than my shitty legs. I hate my legs. In fact, if I had the choice...
jokes.setup: Mike trying to comfort about prosthetic legs
jokes.punchline: Mike's self-deprecating spiral about his own legs
jokes.explanation: Good Mike moment showing his tendency to make things about himself inappropriately

tmdb_overview: As a hostage crisis in Uzbekistan heats up, Selina and Secretary of Defense General Maddox have trouble getting on the same page at their joint appearance at the Marine Corps Base at Quantico. Sue testifies at a congressional hearing on governmental efficiencies; Dan and Gary jockey for the Veep's ear.
insight: Steady escalation carries a mid-tier episode through diplomatic hostage negotiations.
